title: JobOS.ai — Managerial Job Hierarchy Schema
description: Schema for the human-centric 2D orthogonal job model. T-1 anchors purpose (WHY), T-2 anchors outcomes (WHAT), T-3 anchors processes (HOW). The orthogonal axis (Dimension A) captures the experience domain (FEEL). This schema is IP-safe: terminology derived from open JTBD literature (Ulwick, Christensen, Moesta).

placement_2d.description: 2D orthogonal coordinates for the managerial hierarchy. X-axis: functional abstraction level (1=strategic, 5=operational). Y-axis: experience intensity (1=purely functional, 5=highly experiential).

y.description: Experience (Dimension A) intensity axis: 1=purely functional, 5=highly experiential

t1_job.description: T-1 Strategic Job — the overarching purpose (WHY). One per scope. Starts with a goal-oriented verb (Achieve, Ensure, Establish, Build, Grow, Protect, Sustain, Lead).

statement.description: Must start with a goal-oriented action verb. Solution-independent. Addresses a fundamental human or organizational purpose.
examples: Achieve sustainable revenue growth in the mid-market segment
Ensure seamless customer onboarding across all digital channels
Build organizational capability for rapid product iteration

scope_id.description: Logical boundary for Axiom 6 (one root per scope). Typically an organization, product, or program identifier.

rationale.description: Why this is the governing purpose — what imperfection it minimizes at the strategic level.

metrics_hint.description: Suggested outcome metrics (OKR-level). These become ODI outcome statements at T-2.
examples: CAC payback < 18 months
Logo churn < 5% annually

children.description: T-2 Core Functional jobs this T-1 hires. Canonical range: 3–7.

experience_dimension.description: Dimension A (FEEL) nodes orthogonal to this strategic job.

t2_job.description: T-2 Core Functional Job — solution-agnostic desired outcomes (WHAT). Derived from ODI/JTBD outcome statements. Starts with a functional verb (Reduce, Increase, Improve, Minimize, Maximize, Maintain, Ensure).

statement.description: Solution-agnostic outcome statement. Must be measurable in principle. No technology or product names. Ulwick ODI-compatible.
examples: Reduce the time it takes to qualify an inbound lead
Minimize the likelihood of contract renewal failure due to delayed stakeholder alignment
Increase the predictability of quarterly revenue delivery

odi_opportunity_score.description: Ulwick Opportunity Score = importance + max(0, importance - satisfaction). ≥10 = underserved opportunity.

children.description: T-3 Execution jobs that enact this outcome. Canonical range: 3–8.

t3_job.description: T-3 Execution Job — concrete process step or action (HOW). For HUMAN executors: standard imperative statement. For AI executors: T-3 DSL constraint statement (see agent_hierarchy schema for full DSL spec). Starts with an action verb (Define, Locate, Prepare, Execute, Monitor, Adjust, Validate, Report).

statement.description: For HUMAN: plain imperative statement. For AI/MIXED: T-3 DSL constraint (see t3_constraint_dsl).
examples: Define qualification criteria and scoring rubric for inbound leads
Minimize the time to qualify a lead from first contact to CRM stage update, measured in hours, target ≤ 4

standard_step.description: Which of the 8 canonical T3 steps this job corresponds to, if applicable.

t3_constraint.description: Populated when executor_type is AI or MIXED. See t3_constraint_dsl definition.

children.description: Optional T-4 Micro-Jobs. Use for highly granular agent task decomposition.

t4_job.description: T-4 Micro-Job — smallest discrete, self-similar functional action (EXECUTE). Atomic — cannot be meaningfully split. Maps to a single tool call or procedure step.

tool_hint.description: For AI executors: suggested tool or API (e.g., 'CRM.updateStage', 'email.send')

t3_constraint_dsl.description: T-3 DSL constraint for AI/agent executor jobs. Two canonical patterns: Pattern A (time minimization) and Pattern B (likelihood minimization). Both specify the measurable constraint that defines success.

oneOf.title: Pattern A — Time Minimization
oneOf.description: Minimize the time to [verb-noun] from [start_state] to [end_state], measured in [unit], target ≤ [threshold]

verb_noun.description: The action being timed. E.g., 'qualify a lead', 'process an invoice', 'onboard a user'.
examples: qualify a lead
resolve a support ticket
close a purchase order

start_state.description: Observable start condition. E.g., 'first contact', 'ticket created', 'form submitted'.

end_state.description: Observable end condition. E.g., 'CRM stage updated to Qualified', 'ticket closed', 'account active'.

oneOf.title: Pattern B — Likelihood Minimization
oneOf.description: Minimize the likelihood of [event], measured as [rate_type], target ≤ [threshold]

event.description: The undesirable event to minimize. E.g., 'contract renewal failure', 'data entry error', 'SLA breach'.
examples: contract renewal failure due to delayed stakeholder alignment
invoice processing error
customer escalation

measurement_window.description: Time window for rate calculation. E.g., 'per quarter', 'rolling 30 days'.

experience_node.description: Dimension A — Experience/FEEL node. Orthogonal to the functional hierarchy. Captures the emotional or social dimension of job execution for human executors. Statements begin with 'To Be' or 'Feel'.

examples: To Be recognized as a trusted partner
Feel confident in the accuracy of the forecast
To Be free from manual reporting overhead

intensity.description: How central this experience is to job execution. 5=defining, 1=peripheral.

related_job_id.description: The functional job (T1/T2/T3) this experience node is attached to.

description: Root document: a complete Managerial Job Hierarchy.

hierarchy_version.description: Monotonic version counter for change tracking.

scope.description: The organizational or logical scope this hierarchy belongs to.

domain.description: Industry or functional domain. E.g., 'B2B SaaS', 'Healthcare Ops', 'Manufacturing'.

root.description: The single T-1 root job (Axiom 4: Singularity). All T-2 jobs are children.

root.statement: Achieve predictable revenue growth in the SMB segment
root.rationale: SMB segment is underpenetrated; revenue predictability is the primary strategic imperfection.

children.statement: Reduce the time it takes to convert a qualified lead to a closed deal
Minimize the time to complete a deal qualification from discovery call to CRM stage update, measured in hours, target ≤ 24
